user_message: Can you help me prep a brief? The customer (VanArsdel Ltd) is asking about escalation triage.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-10 08:13 EST
Subject: Re: escalation triage Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-05 10:13 EST
Subject: escalation triage - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about escalation triage. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - VanArsdel Ltd
Date: 2024-01-18
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), David (VanArsdel Ltd)

[00:01:15] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is escalation triage.

[00:08:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:11:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] David (VanArsdel Ltd): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:22:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:26:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:31:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-H trap (Skips 'open items from last meeting'); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-H failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-H failure mode (Skips 'open items from last meeting')
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-H -- Skips 'open items from last meeting'
